calendar.matters: MASTER CALENDAR JURY

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-15-2025 Set For Trial On Nov-02-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT SAN MATEO COUNTY HARBOR DISTRICT's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant San Mateo County Harbor District's unopposed motion to compel responses to its first set of form interrogatories, first set of special interrogatories, and first set of requests for production of documents, propounded to plaintiffs Scott Grindy and Anne Grindy, is granted. The court further grants the Harbor District's motion for sanctions.

The Harbor District propounded this discovery separately to each plaintiff on October 1, 2024. (Nozzolino Dec. paras. 2 & 3 & Exs. A and B.) Neither plaintiff has responded. (Id. paras. 9-13.)

By failing to timely respond, Plaintiffs Scott Grindy and Anne Grindy have waived their objections to the discovery requests. (CCP 2030.290(a), 2031.300(a).) Each plaintiff is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, accompanied by responsive documents, within two weeks of notice of entry of this order. If the responsive documents are voluminous plaintiffs may make a rolling production but shall complete production within 30 days.

The Harbor District seeks sanctions. The court concludes that the failure to submit to an authorized method of discovery is a misuse of the discovery process and orders Plaintiffs' counsel Gregory C. Cattermole to pay $1325 to the Harbor District within 30 days of notice of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-21-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA WATER EMERGENCY TRANSPORTATION AUTHORITY's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications to correct a typo:

Defendant San Francisco Bay Area Water Emergency Transportation Authority's unopposed motion to compel responses to its first set of special interrogatories and first set of requests for production of documents is granted. The court further grants the Transportation Authority's motion for sanctions.

The Transportation Authority propounded this discovery on plaintiffs Scott Grindy and Anne Grindy on December 19, 2024. (Cummins Dec. paras. 4 & 5 & Exs. A and B.) Neither plaintiff has responded. (Id. paras. 7-15.)

By failing to timely respond, plaintiffs have waived their objections to the discovery requests. (CCP 2030.290(a), 2031.300(a).) Plaintiffs are ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, accompanied by responsive documents, within two weeks of notice of entry of this order. If the responsive documents are voluminous plaintiffs may make a rolling production but shall complete production within 30 days.

The Transportation Authority seeks sanctions against Plaintiffs and their counsel. The court concludes that the failure to submit to an authorized method of discovery is a misuse of the discovery process and orders that Plaintiffs and their counsel Gregory C. Cattermole jointly and severally shall pay $1575 to the Transportation Authority within 30 days of notice of entry of this order.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
